answer:
**The simile appears in two Sanskrit legal treatises (Smṛtis) in the Falsafa corpus: the *Bṛhaspati Smṛti* (Chapter 1, in a section discussing forged documents and their characteristics, around verse *Brh_1,6.33*) and the *Kātyāyana Smṛti* (Chapter 26, explicitly titled "The Examination of Documents," verse *K_308*).**[^1][^2]

These works, part of the dharmashastra tradition on law and jurisprudence, use nearly identical wording to warn judges against sophisticated forgeries that mimic authentic written instruments (*lekhya*, such as deeds or contracts). The preceding verses in both discuss signs of forgery (e.g., ink quality, handwriting irregularities).[^3][^4]

> Just as a reflection in a mirror, though unreal, appears as real, so skilled persons make copies of documents.[^1]

> Just as an image in a mirror, though unreal, appears real, so do skillful people create copies of documents.[^2]

**Bṛhaspati Smṛti** (1 chapter total): A comprehensive ancient legal text covering disputes, proofs (documents, witnesses), punishments, debts, etc.[^5] The passage follows immediately after listing forgery indicators (e.g., faded ink on an old document, or recent ink on a purportedly ancient one).[^3]

**Kātyāyana Smṛti** (80 chapters): A detailed Smṛti with Chapter 26 devoted to document scrutiny (handwriting verification, scribes, authenticity proofs). It opens: "## The Examination of Documents," with verses on rewriting damaged documents closing it.[^6][^7]

No other works in the corpus match this precise simile in the context of forged legal documents.

[^1]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Chapter 1, [paragraph](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-c67c7e).

[^2]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Chapter 26, [paragraph](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/26/translation/#p-ffea01).

[^3]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Chapter 1, [paragraph](/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-e738b7).

[^4]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Chapter 26, context via chapter wiki.

[^5]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, work wiki.

[^6]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Chapter 26 wiki.

[^7]: *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, work wiki (ch.26 summary).
